name: Governed Isolated Delivery
objective: Convert one independently approved worker task into a bounded Git commit, reproducible validation evidence, and an atomically published namespaced reference without granting the worker lease, commit, validation, or publication authority.
scope: attempt-scoped immutable human authorization of the complete delivery request
atomic hierarchical worktree, file, and artifact lease sets with fencing tokens
isolated detached Git worktrees and pre-created writable roots
bounded worker execution over the union of approved write and artifact scopes
supervisor-owned deterministic commits and clean-checkout independent validation
journal-before-CAS namespaced publication with immutable delivery receipts
restart recovery from durable attempts, lease sets, receipts, and publication journals
fail-closed quarantine, conflict handling, retry isolation, and Git reconciliation markers
rollback compatibility checks and Sprint 04 binary rehearsal
out_of_scope: automatic deletion of quarantined or unverifiably quiescent worktrees
production confidentiality isolation and network egress control
artifact object storage and long-term evidence retention
multi-run worker-pool placement and distributed scheduling
semantic retrieval, graph projection, and context brokering
author: codex
independent_validator: Protected GitHub Actions and isolated Codex CLI review of the public implementation and exact closure candidate
